title: rhus canadensis var. quinata
intro: Rhus canadensis var. quinata, also known as the American Staghorn Sumac, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to eastern North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of tannins and other compounds. The health benefits of Rhus canadensis var. quinata include its use in treating fever, sore throats, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Herbal preparations of Rhus canadensis var. quinata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make medicinal remedies such as salves and poultices.
